exemplar.title: To what extent has Apple leveraged its branding and product innovation to sustain its competitive edge in the global smartphone market ?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Apple leveraged its branding and product innovation to sustain its competitive edge in the global smartphone market ?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently linked to the discussion throughout the essay.
The topic is communicated in a coherent and logical manner, with sufficient background and context to orient the reader.
An appropriate range of secondary sources and recognised Business Management tools (SWOT, STEEPLE, Ansoff Matrix) are employed.
weaknesses: The methodology relies exclusively on secondary sources with uneven academic credibility and lacks clear justification for source selection or appraisal.
Some explanatory passages digress into general company history rather than directly supporting the focus on branding and innovation.
The scope of the investigation remains somewhat broad, limiting the depth of analysis in places.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work sits securely in the middle band (3â4). The research question and topic communication are handled effectively (approaching the top band), but the methodological transparency and focus require further sharpening. An overall score of 4 reflects clear competence with room to refine the methodological rigor and narrow the scope for greater depth.

strengths: Terminology is used accurately and consistently throughout the essay (e.g., âcompetitive advantageâ, âproduct differentiationâ, âAnsoff Matrixâ).
Knowledge of core Business Management concepts is clear and coherent, showing effective understanding of branding and innovation theory.
Source material is applied in appropriate contexts to illustrate key analytical points.
weaknesses: Application of sources is uneven: reliance on journalistic and popular websites undermines academic rigor, and integration with analysis is sometimes descriptive rather than critical.
Subject-knowledge explanations are occasionally superficial, with some unsupported or exaggerated claims.
Citations and evidential detail are at times imprecise, reducing the persuasive power of the argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The criteria for the 3â4 band are broadly met: the essay demonstrates solid knowledge and mostly appropriate use of concepts and terminology, but lacks consistent critical application of source material and occasional imprecision. A score of 4 recognises the clear and coherent subject knowledge while noting areas for deeper engagement with authoritative sources.

strengths: Research is clearly relevant to the research question and organised around established Business Management frameworks.
Analysis shows well-supported conclusions in places, with arguments linked back to empirical examples (e.g., market-share data, campaign case studies).
The discussion acknowledges limitations of the chosen tools and offers a coherent, reasoned argument addressing the extent of Appleâs competitive edge.
weaknesses: The treatment remains largely descriptive; causal links and counter-arguments are not always fully explored.
Quantitative analysis is minimal, and many evaluative comments stop short of deeper critical reflection or alternative perspectives.
Synthesis across different analytical models is limited, and forward-looking recommendations are not developed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Performance aligns with the upper end of the 4â6 band: research is well focused and structured, and analysis leads to supported conclusions, but lacks the sophistication and depth characteristic of higher bands. Awarding 6 reflects strong, relevant critical thinking with identifiable gaps in depth and synthesis.

strengths: All required EE structural elements are present (title page, contents, clear headings, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography).
The layout is generally clean, and the sequence of sections supports reader navigation.
Page numbering and a functional table of contents enhance the reportâs coherence.
weaknesses: Referencing style is inconsistent, with mixed formats, broken URLs and missing publication details.
Typographic and formatting inconsistencies (heading levels, informal expressions) detract from full professional presentation.
Some layout elements (e.g., hierarchical numbering, table formatting) could be improved to strengthen visual clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets the descriptors for the top band (3â4) in presentation: structure and formatting largely support readability and coherence. Minor lapses in referencing consistency and formatting nuances prevent a perfect score, but overall presentation is strong. A score of 4 is therefore justified.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths**: The research question â âTo what extent has Apple leveraged its branding and product innovation to sustain its competitive edge in the global smartphone market?â â is **clearly worded, focused on two specific strategic levers (branding and product innovation) and directly linked to Business Management concepts of competitive advantage**. It is reiterated in the introduction and revisited in the conclusion, showing good integration with the discussion.

**Weaknesses**: Although focused, the question invites a very broad exploration (global market, 2007-present, multiple strategies) that the essay sometimes struggles to cover with depth and supporting data. The scope could have been narrowed further (e.g., to a defined time period or market segment) to sharpen the investigation.

Given the overall clarity and consistent linkage of the question to the analysis, but recognising the slight breadth, the strand fits securely in the 5-6 band; a score of 5 is appropriate rather than 6.

**Strengths**: The conclusion restates answers to the research question and acknowledges limitations of the tools used, showing **some evaluative thinking**. Arguments are generally coherent and traceable to evidence presented in the body.

**Weaknesses**: Evaluation remains **partially critical**; it stops at identifying limitations without exploring their implications or proposing alternative approaches. Counter-arguments (e.g., potential weaknesses in Appleâs strategies) receive limited depth. The reasoning often restates points rather than synthesising them into an integrated judgement.

The discussion satisfies the descriptors for a well-developed but not fully critical argument, placing it in the 4-6 band; a score of 4 reflects its solid yet unexceptional evaluation.

**Strengths**: The student demonstrates a clear grasp of fundamental Business Management concepts such as competitive advantage, branding, product differentiation, Ansoff strategies and STEEPLE factors. Explanations of Appleâs strategic moves (e.g., Face ID, 5G adoption, emotional branding) show an understanding of how these relate to theory.

**Weaknesses**: The knowledge remains **surface-level and occasionally descriptive**, with limited critical depth or acknowledgement of opposing perspectives. Some assertions (e.g., iPhone 12 selling "over 2 billion units in two weeks") appear exaggerated and undermine credibility. Integration of theory with evidence is partial rather than thorough.

Overall, the essay meets the descriptors for the 3-4 band; a score of 3 is awarded to reflect clear but only moderately well-applied subject knowledge.

**Strengths**: Business Management terminologyâsuch as âmarket penetrationâ, âproduct differentiationâ, âcompetitive advantageâ, âSWOTâ, âSTEEPLEâ, âAnsoff Matrixââis used **accurately and consistently** throughout. Definitions are generally correct and terms appear in appropriate contexts.

**Weaknesses**: Minor inaccuracies and instances of informal language (e.g., âgetting the consumer in its clawâ, âchock full of competitionâ) occasionally detract from precision. Citations are not always seamlessly integrated with terminological explanations.

Despite these issues, the accuracy and frequency of correct conceptual usage place the work at the top of the 3-4 band; a score of 4 is justified.

**Strengths**: The student employs SWOT, STEEPLE and Ansoff frameworks to structure the analysis, and links many points back to branding and innovation. There is some attempt to weigh the significance of different factors (e.g., iPhone revenue dependency, geopolitical risks) and to relate strategic tools to Appleâs performance.

**Weaknesses**: Much of the treatment is **descriptive rather than analytical**; statements are often accepted at face value without probing causality or comparing alternative viewpoints. Quantitative analysis is minimal, so conclusions are only partly supported. The essay rarely synthesises findings across tools to build deeper insight.

The work therefore demonstrates clear but limited depth of analysis, meeting the lower end of the 4-6 band; a score of 4 is appropriate.

**Strengths**: The essay cites a wide assortment of articles, reports and corporate documents that are mostly pertinent to Appleâs branding and innovation. Source material is woven into the SWOT, STEEPLE and Ansoff analyses to illustrate key points (e.g., market-share data, campaign descriptions, revenue figures).

**Weaknesses**: Much of the evidence comes from journalistic or promotional pieces (e.g., blogs, LinkedIn articles), and there is limited engagement with academic literature or industry databases. Citations are often descriptive rather than analytically integrated, and data are sometimes outdated or anecdotal. Quantitative support for claims (e.g., market share trends, financial metrics over time) is sparse.

Consequently, the application of sources is **mostly relevant and appropriate but lacks consistency and critical selection**, fitting the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects these mixed qualities.

**Strengths**: The student outlines a methodology centred on secondary research and the application of three recognised Business Management tools (SWOT, STEEPLE and Ansoff Matrix). These frameworks are relevant to analysing branding, innovation and competitive advantage, and the bibliography shows a reasonable variety of sources.

**Weaknesses**: The range of sources is **uneven in academic credibility**, relying heavily on popular websites, blogs and Wikipedia. There is little evidence of rigorous selection criteria or critical appraisal of source reliability. No primary research was attempted, and quantitative data are minimal, limiting triangulation. 

The work therefore demonstrates a generally relevant but not fully effective or well-justified methodological approach, aligning with the 3-4 band; a mid-band score of 3 is awarded.

**Strengths**: The essay draws on a **reasonable breadth of secondary sources** and applies three analytical models to maintain alignment with the research question. Most selected information (e.g., market-share statistics, campaign case studies) is clearly linked to assessing Appleâs competitive edge.

**Weaknesses**: Source quality is mixed, and the absence of primary data or high-level academic studies limits robustness. Some cited facts lack depth or are outdated. Nevertheless, the research remains generally pertinent and supports the investigation in a coherent manner.

These characteristics align best with the 4-6 band for research quality; a mid-band score of 5 reflects clear relevance and organisation, tempered by moderate limitations in rigor.

**Strengths**: Basic layout conventionsâtitle page, page numbering, and a functional table of contentsâare present. Citations and a bibliography exist, demonstrating awareness of academic presentation norms.

**Weaknesses**: Referencing style is **inconsistent and not fully compliant** with IB requirements (e.g., mixed citation formats, broken URLs, missing publication details). Some typographic issues (uneven heading levels, informal HTML tags in tables) and occasional formatting slips detract from professional appearance. 

Mostâbut not allâlayout elements are handled correctly, situating the work in the 1-2 band for this strand; the upper score of 2 is given due to generally readable presentation despite weaknesses.

**Strengths**: The student states the topicâAppleâs use of branding and product innovationâclearly on the title page and reiterates it in the introduction. Background information on Appleâs history, product lines and industry context is provided, giving readers enough orientation to understand why the issue is relevant. 

**Weaknesses**: Explanation, while adequate, is largely descriptive and occasionally digresses into general company history that is not directly linked to the focus on branding and innovation. The depth of contextualization could be stronger, for example by clarifying why these two drivers (branding and innovation) were chosen over others or by defining key constructs more precisely.

Overall, communication is clear and sufficient, matching the descriptors for the 3-4 band; the upper value (4) is awarded because the topic is consistently referenced and understandable throughout the essay.

**Strengths**: The essay contains all mandatory EE structural elements: title page with key details, table of contents, introduction, methodology, main analysis sections, conclusion and bibliography. Headings are clear and logically ordered, enabling smooth navigation and signalling the line of argument.

**Weaknesses**: Transitions between major analytical sections are limited, causing the argument to read as discrete tool applications rather than a fully integrated essay. Nevertheless, the overall organisation satisfies IB expectations for clarity and coherence.

Given that all structural requirements are met and the sequence aids comprehension, the work merits the top of the 3-4 band; a score of 4 is awarded.

annotations.comment: The inclusion of a precise word count at the front of the essay fulfils IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to presentation details.
The investigation title is clearly stated at the start of the report, meeting the IB requirement for report identification and aiding reader orientation.
Page numbers are missing on the title page footer; ensure consistent pagination throughout the report to comply with IB formatting standards.
The methodology describes secondary research but lacks criteria for source selection and appraisal. Define how sources were evaluated for relevance and credibility.
The claim of 2 billion units sold in two weeks is unrealistic; verify the Gallagher citation and correct the figure or timing to maintain credibility.
The campaign description lacks evaluation of its effectiveness (e.g., ROI, engagement metrics). Incorporate quantitative performance indicators to strengthen analysis.
The threats section remains general. Compare Appleâs vulnerability directly against specific competitorsâ strategies to deepen critical insight.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent citation formats and missing publisher details. Adopt a single referencing style (e.g., APA or MLA) and apply it uniformly.
Several URLs are broken across lines and lack proper hyperlink formatting. Ensure web addresses are complete and accessible, with uniform formatting.
The table of contents is functional but would benefit from hierarchical numbering of sections and subsections to strengthen the structural clarity of the report.
Consider improving the formatting of the table entries (e.g., alignment or tab leaders) to enhance readability and visual consistency.
Citing Wikipedia for Appleâs industry position undermines academic rigor; replace with peerâreviewed articles or industry reports and integrate data from authoritative sources.
The claim that the iPhone accounts for 50% of revenue lacks context and supporting figures. Include a table or chart with up-to-date financial data to substantiate this assertion.
The use of first-person narrative (âI opted to conduct researchâ¦â) reduces formality. Recast in an impersonal academic tone to align with extended essay conventions.
STEEPLE analysis omits the legal factor without explanation. Justify the exclusion or discuss potential legal issues to strengthen methodological transparency.
The discussion of the Ansoff Matrix is too general. Specify what data (e.g., sales, market share) will feed into each quadrant and how it will be analysed.
The SWOT table headings are concise but lack evidential support. Expand each point with data or citations to illustrate why each factor is classified as a strength, weakness, opportunity, or threat.
The narrative of Steve Jobsâ 2007 launch is descriptive. Link these events directly to branding or innovation concepts and critically analyse their strategic impact.
Market-share data from 2007 is outdated; update the analysis with recent figures or trends to ensure relevance to the current competitive landscape.
The phrase âgetting the consumer in its clawâ is informal. Replace colloquial expressions with precise business terminology to maintain academic tone.
The pricing comparison lacks a source for the average market price. Cite market-research data or industry reports to substantiate claims about premium pricing as a weakness.
Reliance on a single quarterly figure for iPhone revenue offers limited insight. Analyse revenue trends over multiple years to identify patterns and risks more robustly.
Supply-chain risks are described but not quantified. Include estimates of cost impact or probability of disruption to sharpen the threat assessment.
Regulatory challenges are outlined but lack discussion of financial or operational consequences. Evaluate the potential fines or market restrictions to illustrate real impact.
When discussing technology factors, include specific R&D expenditure figures or patent counts to demonstrate the scale of Appleâs innovation investment.
The political/economic analysis uses vague idioms. Replace colloquial phrases with clear, formal descriptions of macroeconomic influences.
Market-penetration discussion references partnerships but omits data on how these arrangements increased sales. Provide statistics on subscriber uptake or regional share gains.
Product differentiation examples are listed but not compared against competitor features. Critically assess how unique each innovation was relative to market alternatives.
The concluding synthesis restates framework findings but does not integrate insights across models. Draw connections between SWOT, STEEPLE and Ansoff to develop a cohesive final argument.
Limitations are identified but lack forward-looking recommendations. Suggest specific avenues for future quantitative research or primary data collection.
The conclusionâs descriptive summary of brand image would benefit from critical reflection on how these perceptions compare with consumer sentiment studies or brand equity metrics.
Discussion of methodological limitations is sound but could be improved by suggesting alternative approaches (e.g., surveys, interviews) to address identified biases.
The bibliography is not alphabetised by author surname. Reorder entries alphabetically to conform with standard academic presentation practices.
